rules.statement: Numeric confidence scores and decision thresholds used as operation gates in Will-tier logic MUST be loaded from operational_config.yaml via load_operational_config(), not hardcoded as float literals in src/. Per ADR-040: no hardcoded operational values in src/.
rules.rationale: ADR-040 declared operational_config.yaml as the single canonical home for all runtime numeric values. Hardcoded thresholds in strategists and orchestrators mean a threshold change requires a code edit, review, and deployment rather than a config edit. It also breaks the audit trail: a governance decision about acceptable confidence levels is invisible when buried in a return statement or dict literal. Compliant pattern: from shared.infrastructure.intent.operational_config import load_operational_config; _CFG = load_operational_config().section; then _CFG.field. See complexity_strategist.py and clarity_strategist.py for established precedent.
